refinement_sections.content: We need to create a computer program that can look at English sentences and understand their structure. This means it should be able to tell what parts of the sentence are nouns, verbs, adjectives, etc. The main goal is to work with normal statements but also include questions and commands.

The program should work with different types of sentences, especially simple ones. It should also be able to analyze sentences quickly enough to be useful. We need to include some recent ideas from linguistics, but I'm not sure what those are yet.
We don't have a specific set of sentences to use, so we will need to come up with our own examples. I think we can use common sentences that people use every day.

We need to think about how to check if the program is working well. This might include seeing how many sentences it gets right compared to what a human would say. I'm not sure what the industry standards are for this.
We should write a short report that explains how we made the program and what choices we made. This should also include any assumptions we had about the sentences we chose.
